title: Mermaid diagram preview component with theme support and editing capabilities
purpose: Renders Mermaid diagrams with theme support, error handling, zoom, pan, and edit mode functionality. Used to visualize diagram syntax in a web UI.
module_context: Exports a React component MermaidPreview that handles diagram rendering, theme switching, zoom/pan interactions, and edit mode callbacks. Includes imperative methods for centering and fitting diagrams, and manages loading states with error handling.

steps.content: Reset the pan offset to center the diagram.
Set the zoom level to 100%.
Calculate the available space in the wrapper container.
Determine the SVG dimensions of the current diagram.
Compute the zoom level needed to fit the diagram within the available space.
Clamp the computed zoom level to a reasonable range between 25% and 400%.
Reset the pan offset and apply the calculated zoom level.
Prevent the default scroll behavior when Ctrl or Cmd key is pressed.
IF the scroll direction is up, trigger the zoom in callback.
ELSE trigger the zoom out callback.
IF the middle mouse button is pressed or shift+left-click is detected, start panning.
Store the initial mouse position and current pan offset.
Set the cursor to grabbing to indicate panning is active.
IF panning is active, calculate the new pan offset based on mouse movement.
Update the pan offset state with the calculated values.
IF panning was active, stop panning and reset the cursor.
IF the shift key is still pressed, set cursor to grab.
ELSE reset cursor to default.
IF panning is active, prevent the default context menu from appearing.
IF the shift key is pressed and panning is not active, set cursor to grab.
IF the shift key is released and panning is not active, reset cursor to default.
Walk up the DOM tree from the clicked element to find a node or edge.
IF a node element is found, trigger the node click callback with the node ID.
ELSE IF an edge element is found, trigger the edge click callback with the edge ID.
Check if the clicked element is a node using diagram utility functions.
IF a node is identified, trigger the node click with position callback.
ELSE check if the clicked element is an edge using diagram utility functions.
IF an edge is identified, trigger the edge click with position callback.
IF the content is empty, set loading state to false and clear any error.
Set loading state to true and clear any previous error.
Generate a unique render ID to prevent caching issues.
Render the Mermaid diagram using the mermaid library.
IF the container ref exists, update its inner HTML with the rendered SVG.
Set loading state to false and clear any error after successful render.
IF rendering fails, capture the error message and update state with error.
